title: discospermum parvifolium
intro: Discospermum parvifolium, also known as the Khasi orange, is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. It is a shrub or small tree native to the tropical regions of Asia, and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant has been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and protect against infections, thereby providing several health benefits. The herbal preparations of Discospermum parvifolium can be made into infusions, decoctions, or extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues, skin conditions, and digestive problems.
